title.zh: 腸道幹細胞「對稱分裂」驚人發現：為何我們的腸子每週重製自己？
title.en: The Intestine's Secret: How Symmetric Stem Cell Division Rebuilds Us Weekly

summary.zh:
你每天攝取的食物，其實正被腸道幹細胞以不可思議的速度「重製」——這片全球最快速更新的成人組織，每1-2週就完成一次全面換血。腸道黏膜就像一座精準運轉的微型城市，隱藏在腸壁深處的『腸隱窩』（crypt）與突起的『絨毛』（villus）構成網格，讓科學家能像搭積木般觀察細胞如何分工合作：吸收營養、分泌黏液、抵禦細菌，甚至調節血糖的GLP-1激素。過去我們以為幹細胞像『獨角獸』般單獨工作，但最新研究揭開驚人真相：這些幹細胞竟像永不停歇的建築工人，持續對稱分裂，讓每個新生細胞都帶著『幹細胞潛能』。

更震撼的是，這些幹細胞能在實驗室中無限增殖，而原本分化成腸道細胞的『女兒細胞』，遇到傷害時竟會『回鍋』重當幹細胞，重新編寫再生規則。這就像城市在颱風過後，消防員能轉行當建築師，工程師能當救護員——體內的細胞正展現出令人驚嘆的『細胞流動性』，為再生醫學打開全新視野。
summary.en:
Your daily meal is being 'recreated' by intestinal stem cells at a pace that defies imagination—the fastest-renewing adult mammalian tissue in the body, with a complete turnover every 1-2 weeks. The intestinal epithelium functions like a precision-engineered micro-city, where the crypt-villus architecture forms a grid-like structure allowing scientists to observe cellular teamwork: nutrient absorption, mucus production, bacterial defense, and even glucose regulation via GLP-1 hormones. For decades, stem cells were thought to act like solitary unicorns, but new research reveals a stunning truth: these cells behave like tireless construction workers, dividing symmetrically to ensure every new cell retains stem-like potential.

Even more remarkable, these stem cells can proliferate indefinitely in the lab, while differentiated 'daughter cells' can revert to stem cells during regeneration—a cellular version of firefighters becoming architects and engineers becoming paramedics after a storm. This unprecedented plasticity challenges long-held assumptions about tissue maintenance and opens new pathways for regenerative medicine, revealing a biological choreography far more dynamic and resilient than we ever imagined.

keyArticle.title: Shifting paradigms in tissue stem cell biology: Insights from the intestine
